name: ETH Squeeze Persistence v1
thesis: ETH 4h frequently compresses before directional bursts, but the useful signal is persistence after release rather than the first breakout print. The edge should come from broader release-plus-hold behavior with active de-risking.
invalidation_condition: Abort if this remains too sparse, internally contradictory, or cannot produce enough blind-window trades after costs.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Compression regime that transitions into directional follow-through after a release bar actually holds.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Breakouts from low-volatility clusters are common, but the durable edge comes from staying with the ones that retain structure rather than fading immediately.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 4h routinely forms clean volatility contractions and expansions without excessive intrabar noise.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If breakout persistence is too rare in this lane, the hold filter may still suppress participation.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If trade count is low but setup quality looks coherent, relax the persistence threshold before changing the compression logic.

density_sanity_check.plausible_trade_count: low-to-moderate but acceptable for 4h if compression threshold is broad
density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; compression, hold, and momentum all align post-release
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by widening band-width threshold and modest ADX floor

long: Time-based de-risk after 3 bars if unrealized gain < 0.35 * ATR_14
short: Time-based de-risk after 3 bars if unrealized gain < 0.35 * ATR_14
